01_initial_request.input_data: GPR110 C-terminal fragment constructs, G-protein subunits (Gq, Gs, Gi, G12, G13), Sf9 insect cells
01_initial_request.user_intent: Elucidate the structural mechanism of GPR110 self-activation by stalk peptide and investigate G-protein coupling selectivity
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: High-resolution cryo-EM structures (2.66–3.09 Å) and quantitative measurements of G-protein coupling activity (e.g., reporter assay RLU, BRET ratios)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] GPR110 C-terminal fragment constructs needed for structural studies. [Gap] Expression constructs must be designed for baculovirus system. [Decision] To achieve protein expression, chose to use pFastBac1 baculovirus expression vector to perform cloning of GPR110 gene (residues 567-873) with LgBiT fusion, expecting to obtain expression-ready construct.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: GPR110 CTF construct successfully cloned for baculovirus expression

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] G-protein subunits needed for complex formation. [Gap] Multiple Gα subunit constructs required for different coupling studies. [Decision] To achieve G-protein complex assembly, chose to use pFastBac plasmid to perform cloning of Gβ1 HiBiT fusion, mini-Gαq, mini-Gαs, Gα12, Gα13, and Gαi constructs, expecting to obtain expression-ready G-protein constructs.

parameters.Gα12_construct: GGSGG linker swapped with Gαi AHD domain, first 19 residues substituted with first 18 residues of Gαi
parameters.Gα13_construct: AHD swapped with Gαi AHD, first 19 residues replaced with first 18 residues of Gαi

02_agent_trajectory.observation: G-protein subunit constructs successfully cloned

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein expression required for complex purification. [Gap] Receptor and G-protein subunits need to be co-expressed. [Decision] To achieve complex formation, chose to use Baculovirus expression system to perform co-infection of Sf9 cells with receptor, Gα, Gβ, and Gγ, expecting to obtain expressed protein complexes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: GPR110/G-protein complexes successfully expressed in Sf9 cells

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Nb35 nanobody needed for Gs and Gq complex stabilization. [Gap] Nb35 must be expressed separately. [Decision] To achieve Nb35 production, chose to use E. coli BL21 expression system to perform Nb35 expression in periplasm, expecting to obtain purified Nb35 for complex stabilization.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Nb35 successfully expressed and purified by nickel affinity chromatography

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Membrane proteins need to be solubilized for purification. [Gap] GPR110/G-protein complexes embedded in membrane must be extracted. [Decision] To achieve protein solubilization, chose to use detergent solubilization with LMNG and CHS to perform membrane extraction, expecting to obtain solubilized protein complexes.

parameters.buffer: 20 mM Hepes, 150 mM NaCl, 10 mM MgCl2, 20 mM KCl, 5 mM CaCl2, pH 7.5

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Membrane proteins successfully solubilized

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Solubilized proteins need affinity purification. [Gap] MBP-tagged GPR110 must be purified from cell lysate. [Decision] To achieve initial purification, chose to use amylose column chromatography to perform affinity purification of MBP-tagged receptor, expecting to obtain enriched GPR110/G-protein complexes.

parameters.wash_buffer: 20 mM Hepes, pH 7.5, 150 mM NaCl, 0.01% LMNG, 0.002% CHS

02_agent_trajectory.observation: GPR110/G-protein complexes enriched by affinity purification

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Affinity-purified complexes need further purification. [Gap] TEV cleavage and size separation required. [Decision] To achieve final purification, chose to use Superdex 200 Increase 10/300 GL gel filtration to perform size exclusion chromatography, expecting to obtain homogeneous GPR110/G-protein complexes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Homogeneous GPR110/G-protein complexes obtained for cryo-EM

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Purified complexes need to be prepared for cryo-EM imaging. [Gap] Samples must be vitrified on grids. [Decision] To achieve grid preparation, chose to use Vitrobot Mark IV to perform grid vitrification, expecting to obtain frozen-hydrated samples for data collection.

parameters.grid_type: glow-charged quantifoil R1.2/1.3 Cu holey carbon grids

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cryo-EM grids successfully prepared with evenly distributed particles

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Grids need to be imaged for structure determination. [Gap] High-resolution cryo-EM data must be collected. [Decision] To achieve data collection, chose to use FEI 300kV Titan Krios TEM to perform cryo-EM imaging, expecting to obtain high-quality micrographs for structure determination.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cryo-EM images collected for all five G-protein complexes

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw cryo-EM movies need motion correction. [Gap] Beam-induced motion must be corrected. [Decision] To achieve motion correction, chose to use MotionCor2 to perform frame alignment and binning, expecting to obtain motion-corrected micrographs.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Motion-corrected micrographs obtained for all complexes

references.relevance: MotionCor2 algorithm for beam-induced motion correction

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Motion-corrected movies need CTF estimation. [Gap] Contrast transfer function parameters must be determined. [Decision] To achieve CTF estimation, chose to use CTFFIND 4.1 to perform CTF parameter estimation, expecting to obtain defocus and astigmatism values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: CTF parameters estimated for all micrographs

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Particles need to be picked from micrographs. [Gap] Individual protein particles must be identified. [Decision] To achieve particle picking, chose to use crYOLO to perform automated particle picking, expecting to obtain particle coordinates for extraction.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Particles picked from all micrographs

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Picked particles need 2D classification. [Gap] Poor quality particles must be removed. [Decision] To achieve particle cleaning, chose to use RELION 3.1 to perform reference-free 2D classification, expecting to obtain good particle classes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Good particle classes selected for 3D reconstruction

references.relevance: RELION 3.1 for automated cryo-EM structure determination

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Initial 3D model needed for refinement. [Gap] Ab initio reconstruction required. [Decision] To achieve initial model generation, chose to use cryoSPARC to perform ab initio reconstruction, expecting to obtain initial 3D model for classification.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Initial 3D model generated

references.relevance: cryoSPARC for rapid unsupervised cryo-EM structure determination

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Particles need 3D classification. [Gap] Heterogeneous particles must be separated. [Decision] To achieve 3D classification, chose to use RELION 3.1 to perform 3D classification with 5 classes, expecting to obtain homogeneous particle subset.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Best 3D class selected for refinement

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Selected particles need 3D refinement. [Gap] High-resolution reconstruction required. [Decision] To achieve 3D refinement, chose to use RELION 3.1 to perform 3D refinement with Bayesian polishing and CTF refinement, expecting to obtain improved map quality.

parameters.refinement_steps: 3D refinement, Bayesian polishing, 3D refinement, CTF refinement

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Refined particle set obtained

references.relevance: Bayesian approach to beam-induced motion correction

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Refined particles need final reconstruction. [Gap] Non-uniform refinement for best resolution. [Decision] To achieve final reconstruction, chose to use cryoSPARC Non-uniform Refinement to perform final 3D refinement, expecting to obtain high-resolution cryo-EM maps.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Final cryo-EM maps obtained with resolutions: Gq 2.85 Å, Gs 2.84 Å, Gi 3.09 Å, G12 2.8 Å, G13 2.66 Å

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cryo-EM maps need post-processing. [Gap] Map quality enhancement required. [Decision] To achieve map enhancement, chose to use DeepEMhancer to perform cryo-EM volume post-processing, expecting to obtain improved map quality for model building.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Post-processed cryo-EM maps obtained

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Atomic models need to be built into cryo-EM maps. [Gap] Initial models required for docking. [Decision] To achieve initial model placement, chose to use UCSF Chimera to perform model docking into electron microscopy density maps, expecting to obtain positioned initial models.

parameters.initial_models: AlphaFold structures of human GPR110 (AF-Q5T6I0-F1), Gi protein complex from D2R (PDB 6vms and 7jvr), Gs protein complex from M1R (PDB 7f4q)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Initial models docked into cryo-EM density maps

references.relevance: UCSF Chimera for visualization and docking

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Docked models need manual adjustment. [Gap] Model-map fit requires refinement. [Decision] To achieve model optimization, chose to use Coot to perform iterative manual adjustment, expecting to obtain improved model-map fit.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Manually adjusted atomic models obtained

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Manually adjusted models need computational refinement. [Gap] Rosetta refinement for cryo-EM data. [Decision] To achieve model refinement, chose to use Rosetta cryoEM refinement to perform relax model refinement, expecting to obtain energetically favorable models.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Rosetta-refined models obtained

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Rosetta-refined models need real-space refinement. [Gap] Phenix refinement for final optimization. [Decision] To achieve final refinement, chose to use Phenix real space refinement to perform model optimization against cryo-EM maps, expecting to obtain final atomic models.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Final refined atomic models obtained

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Final models need validation. [Gap] Model quality must be assessed. [Decision] To achieve model validation, chose to use MolProbity to perform all-atom structure validation, expecting to obtain validation statistics.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Model statistics validated

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Structural observations need functional validation. [Gap] Receptor activity must be measured. [Decision] To achieve functional validation, chose to use Dual-Glo Luciferase system to perform reporter assays (NFAT-RE, CRE, SRE, SRF-RE), expecting to obtain quantitative receptor activity measurements.

parameters.transfection_reagent: X-tremeGENE HP (Roche) at ratio 3:1 to DNA

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Reporter assay data showing GPR110 CTF activates all 4 major G-protein signaling pathways; stalk peptide deletion abrogates activity

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] G-protein coupling needs quantitative measurement in physiological setting. [Gap] Constitutive activity must be assessed. [Decision] To achieve coupling quantification, chose to use BRET assay with tricistronic activity sensors to perform quantitative measurement of GPR110 constitutive activity, expecting to obtain G-protein coupling strength data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: BRET data showing Gq, G13, and Gs have strong coupling activity; Gi has weakest coupling activity

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Stalk peptide interaction needs computational validation. [Gap] Hydrogen bond formation between S570stalk and H8207,39 unclear from static structure. [Decision] To achieve interaction validation, chose to use CHARMM-GUI to perform MD simulation system setup with POPC:cholesterol membrane, expecting to obtain simulation-ready system.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: MD simulation system prepared

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] MD system needs production simulation. [Gap] S570stalk-H8207,39 hydrogen bond formation must be tested. [Decision] To achieve interaction analysis, chose to use Amber20 to perform molecular dynamics simulation with three independent 200 ns runs, expecting to obtain trajectory data for hydrogen bond analysis.

parameters.cutoff: 10 Å for short-range electrostatic and van der Waals
parameters.constraints: SHAKE algorithm for bonds with hydrogens

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MD simulation shows S570stalk and H8207,39 form hydrogen bond, confirming polar interaction contribution

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Physiological relevance needs to be tested. [Gap] Stalk peptide effect on neurons must be examined. [Decision] To achieve physiological validation, chose to use primary cortical neuron preparation to perform neurite outgrowth assay, expecting to obtain evidence of stalk peptide activation in physiological context.

parameters.treatments: 1 μM DHA, 100 μM stalk-peptide, or vehicle

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Stalk peptide promotes neurite outgrowth similar to DHA effect

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Neurite outgrowth needs quantification. [Gap] Neurons must be visualized and measured. [Decision] To achieve neurite measurement, chose to use immunocytochemistry with anti-MAP2 antibody to perform neuron staining and imaging, expecting to obtain quantitative neurite outgrowth data.

parameters.blocking: 0.2% Triton X-100 and 10% goat serum for 1 h
parameters.microscope: Zeiss LSM880 laser scanning confocal microscope
parameters.analysis: 60-120 neurons per group using ImageJ, 5 images with 4-8 MAP2-positive neurons per image

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Quantitative neurite outgrowth data showing stalk peptide effect similar to DHA

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Cryo-EM structure determination combined with functional assays (reporter assays, BRET assay, neurite outgrowth assay)

cryo_EM_resolution_Gi.interpretation: Structure of GPR110/Gi complex with weaker density on H8

cryo_EM_resolution_G13.interpretation: Highest resolution structure of GPR110/G13 complex

G_protein_interface_area_Gq.interpretation: Receptor interface area for Gq engagement

G_protein_interface_area_Gs.interpretation: Receptor interface area for Gs engagement

G_protein_interface_area_Gi.interpretation: Smallest receptor interface area, explains poor Gi coupling

G_protein_interface_area_G12.interpretation: Largest receptor interface area for G12 engagement

G_protein_interface_area_G13.interpretation: Receptor interface area for G13 engagement

reporter_assay_activity.interpretation: GPR110 CTF activates all 4 major G-protein signaling pathways; stalk peptide deletion abrogates activity

BRET_coupling_strength.interpretation: Gq, G13, and Gs have strong coupling; Gi has weakest coupling, consistent with structural observations

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved: High-resolution cryo-EM structures (2.66-3.09 Å) of GPR110 in complex with all 5 G-proteins (Gq, Gs, Gi, G12, G13) were obtained. Structural mechanism of GPR110 self-activation by stalk peptide was elucidated through ligand binding pocket analysis and mutagenesis validation. G-protein coupling selectivity was investigated through systematic comparison revealing Class I (Gq/Gs) vs Class II (Gi/G12/G13) engagement patterns based on αH5 residue characteristics at position -4. Physiological relevance confirmed through BRET assay and neurite outgrowth experiments showing stalk peptide activates GPR110 in primary neurons.
